commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=bd8543a942d02dcbade9e66af0a16706a7ab3ead)
commands.review_summary:
The PR reuses existing default-account warning helpers in doctor preview, adds a core doctor health check for lint, and adds regression tests for preview and health-check detection.

PR surface: Source +33, Tests +74. Total +107 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: `runDoctorLintCli` uses contribution-derived checks, and PR head adds the new check only to `CORE_HEALTH_CHECKS`. I did not run tests because this review is read-only, but the existing contribution invariant shows the failure path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Hold for human review until the lint-registration defect is fixed and the contributor adds real CLI behavior proof; automation should not repair while the external proof gate is mock-only.

target.title: fix(doctor): surface multi-account default routing warnings in preview and lint
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where operators configuring multiple channel accounts (for example Telegram `alerts` + `work`) would see default-routing warnings during full `openclaw doctor`, but **not** during non-repair preview mode or `openclaw doctor --lint`. That left a silent misconfiguration path: CI/automation and preview-only doctor runs could pass while account routing was still ambiguous.

## Why This Change Was Made

The warning helpers already existed in `src/commands/doctor/shared/default-account-warnings.ts` and were wired into the full doctor config flow (`doctor-config-flow.ts`), but preview aggregation (`collectDoctorPreviewNotes`) and lint health checks never called them. This change reuses the same helpers in both paths and adds a dedicated lint health check `core/doctor/default-account-routing` so preview and `--lint` stay aligned with full doctor output.

## User Impact

Operators and automation using `openclaw doctor` preview (non-`--fix`) or `openclaw doctor --lint` now get the same multi-account default-routing warnings as full doctor for:
- missing `accounts.default` with incomplete account-scoped bindings
- multiple configured accounts without an explicit default

No runtime routing behavior changes; diagnostics only.

## Evidence

**Before (on `origin/main`):** `collectDoctorPreviewNotes` and `doctor --lint` health checks did not import `collectMissingDefaultAccountBindingWarnings` / `collectMissingExplicitDefaultAccountWarnings`.

**After:** targeted regression tests pass with real multi-account Telegram fixture.

Commands run locally (Linux, Node 22.22.0):

```bash
CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.test.ts \
  src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts
```

Observed:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  16 passed (16)
...
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  44 passed (44)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 13.19s
```

New behavior proof (verbose):

```bash
CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.test.ts \
  src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts \
  -t "missing default account|default account routing" --reporter=verbose
```

Observed:

```text
✓ doctor preview warnings > collects missing default account routing warnings in preview mode
✓ CORE_HEALTH_CHECKS > reports missing default account routing as core health findings
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  2 passed | 58 skipped (60)
```

Sibling helper coverage (unchanged, still green):

```bash
CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/commands/doctor-config-flow.missing-default-account-bindings.integration.test.ts \
  src/commands/doctor-config-flow.missing-default-account-bindings.test.ts \
  src/commands/doctor-config-flow.missing-explicit-default-account.test.ts
```

Observed:

```text
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  15 passed (15)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 5.52s
```

**Files touched:** 4 files, +110 / -3 LOC (prod + tests).

Marked AI-assisted.
